title: New U.S. Missile Hit Iranian Sports Hall and School, Analysis Shows
summary: The Pentagon used missiles untested in combat in a deadly attack that struck civilian sites near a military compound on Feb. 28, according to visual evidence examined by The Times and weapons experts.

title: Trump Claims ‘Regime Change’ in Iran Is Already Complete
summary: President Trump suggested that Iran’s current leaders were “much more reasonable” as he sought to show progress in his war aims. Iran’s power structure appears firmly in control despite a month of U.S.-Israeli attacks.

title: T.S.A. Lines Appear to Be Getting Shorter as Workers Begin to Receive Paychecks
summary: The hourslong waits at airports appear to be easing after the president signed an order to pay Transportation Security Administration officers.

title: Michael Rousseau, Air Canada’s CEO, to Step Down Amid Backlash Over Comments After Crash
summary: Michael Rousseau’s mostly English statement after a fatal runway collision drew sharp criticism. The airline said it would emphasize French fluency in its search for a successor.

title: Who’s ‘Safer’ or ‘Riskier’? Maine Democrats Clash Over How to Beat Collins.
summary: Gov. Janet Mills argues that her rival for Senate, Graham Platner, could be doomed by his history of offensive online remarks. But at a time of anti-establishment anger, Mr. Platner says he is the safer choice.

title: ‘I Think That MAGA Is Dying:’ Inside the Youth Movement at CPAC
summary: At a sparsely attended Conservative Political Action Conference, young Republicans were eager to start the post-Trump era.

title: A Democratic Electrician Nabs a State Senate Seat in Republican Florida
summary: With Brian Nathan’s victory certified, Democrats won two of three state legislative races in this month’s special elections, all in Republican-leaning districts.

title: Israel Passes Law to Allow Hanging of Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
summary: The law could be applied to Jewish Israeli extremists, but experts say that will likely never happen.

title: Israel Pulls Battalion From West Bank After Soldier Talks of ‘Revenge’
summary: While detaining a CNN crew, a soldier said that Israeli troops were motivated by “revenge” against Palestinians and that all of the West Bank was “for the Jews.”

title: Doctors Believed Woody Brown Would Never Understand Language. He’s Publishing a Novel.
summary: Doctors believed that Woody Brown would never be able to speak or process language. He went to graduate school and is publishing his debut novel.

title: Private-Credit Wobbles Could Prove Perilous for Trump
summary: The Trump administration is poised to broaden access to risky investments that are showing signs of strain.

title: Justice Dept. Watchdog Has Gone Silent, Lawyers for Whistle-Blower Say
summary: Lawyers for a fired Justice Department lawyer say the agency’s inspector general appears to have ignored at least 20 different requests to scrutinize misconduct.

title: Texas Border Wall Plan in Big Bend Draws Opposition From Republicans and Democrats
summary: Plans for a wall in Big Bend National Park prompted public protests and behind-the-scenes lobbying to sway the Trump administration to stop.

title: Trump’s Birthright Citizenship Order at Supreme Court Splits Conservative Scholars
summary: Before President Trump’s order to limit birthright citizenship, there was widespread agreement that the 14th Amendment guaranteed citizenship for U.S.-born babies.

title: Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse Paintings Are Stolen in 3-Minute Museum Heist, Police Say
summary: Thieves broke into the Magnani-Rocca Foundation outside Parma, Italy, officials said, and made off with paintings worth millions.

title: How to Watch and See the Artemis II Moon Launch
summary: The first crewed mission around the moon in more than 50 years is coming up. Here’s how to see it at sites in and around the Kennedy Space Center in Florida or online.

title: NASA Is Launching Astronauts to the Moon, but Americans Aren’t That Excited
summary: Polling has consistently found that most people would prefer NASA spend money on things like monitoring climate change and averting asteroid collisions rather than human spaceflight.

title: How a Civics School With a Conservative Bent Divided Its Supporters
summary: A University of North Carolina program was intended to promote civil discourse and ideological diversity. Some of its early conservative supporters say it is doing the opposite.

title: How Bard College Became a Major Real Estate Player 20 Miles Away
summary: A nonprofit foundation donated $82 million worth of properties in Hudson, N.Y., to the school, which has provided few details about its plans.

title: The Dogma of Meat
summary: From Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s food pyramid to online influencers, beef has become more than just a source of protein.

title: Can You Fake Being Rich?
summary: A spate of popular social media accounts offer advice on how to achieve an “old-money” look.

title: The Epstein Class Had a Signature Weakness
summary: Epstein flourished by helping elite men reconcile their private desires with their public appearances.

title: Democrats Need a New Promise: A House by 30
summary: The federal government can once again make building wealth for young families a core mission.

title: Your Questions About the News
summary: Times reporters answer reader questions about the war, the economy and more.

title: How Russia Weaponized the Cold Ukrainian Winter
summary: Inside one Kyiv neighborhood as it braved the harshest conditions since World War II.

title: 2 Rikers Detainees Die as Mamdani Faces Deadline to Shut Troubled Jail
summary: Two men who had been held on Rikers Island died in the past week, and Mayor Zohran Mamdani he would “pursue every avenue” to shut the troubled facility as soon as possible.

title: Luis Carlos Rúa Fought Colombia’s Corruption in an Elephant Costume. Now He’s a Senator.
summary: After years of anonymously documenting abandoned public works projects in Colombia, Luis Carlos Rúa revealed himself days before his election.
